title: get_bbox() x==0/y==0 skip is a fragile heuristic
description: endrawing.py:112-113 skips any element whose placement origin has x==0.0 or y==0.0. Intended to drop unplaced origin elements, but it silently discards legitimately placed elements landing on either world axis (e.g. building grid origin on x=0). Combined with the <2-element crash, over-filtering here can crash get_bbox. Use 'x==0 and y==0 and z==0' (only skip true origin) or detect identity/default placement.
close_reason: Fixed get_bbox origin skip and <2-element crash; added tests

title: get_bbox() crashes with fewer than 2 valid elements
description: In GeometryUtils.get_bbox (endrawing.py:94-138), bbox_min and bbox_max start as [] and fill lazily (bbox_min on the 1st valid element, bbox_max on the 2nd). If a location yields 0 or 1 qualifying element, bbox_max stays [] and the midpoint calc at line 132 (bbox_max[0]) throws IndexError. A building with a single placed element, or one where everything is filtered by the x==0/y==0 rule, hard-crashes. Needs a guard.
close_reason: Fixed get_bbox origin skip and <2-element crash; added tests

title: Storeys at identical elevation collide in storeys dict
description: endrawing.py:840-847 keys storeys[local_placement[2][3]] = ifc_storey by Z. Two storeys at the same elevation (mezzanines, split levels) silently overwrite, losing a drawing. Key by storey GlobalId and sort by elevation separately.

title: get_centroid() has no failure handling for geometry-less spaces
description: ifcopenshell.geom.create_shape() (endrawing.py:150-151) raises if a space has no geometry representation. create_space_labels calls get_centroid for every space without try/except, so one geometry-less space aborts the whole run. Wrap and skip on failure.

title: get_bbox() measures placement origins, not geometry extents
description: endrawing.py:103-110 computes the bbox from each element's local-placement translation only. True footprint extends beyond outermost insertion point by wall length/thickness, so bbox is undersized and cameras can clip building edges. Related to but distinct from the rotation FIXME at line 834. Use ifcopenshell.geom bounds or actual shape vertices.

title: natsorted building sort assumes Name is set
description: endrawing.py:276-278 sorts buildings with key=lambda x: x.Name. A building with Name==None breaks natsort comparison. Use 'x.Name or empty-string' fallback.
